message:
fix(bigquery): restrict base_url host_allow to bigquery.googleapis.com only

Narrow the BigQuery connector's network allowlist so it can only talk to the
single Google endpoint it actually needs. Previously the policy accepted
`bigquery.googleapis.com`, any `*.googleapis.com` host, and the three
localhost aliases used by tests. The wildcard branch was wider than this
connector's actual call surface and gave capability holders a silent path
to reach unrelated Google services (content-*.googleapis.com,
www.googleapis.com, iam.googleapis.com, etc.) through the same token.

Changes:

- connectors/bigquery/src/client.rs `normalize_base_url` drops the
  `ends_with(".googleapis.com")` branch, so only the exact bigquery
  host and the local test hosts (localhost, 127.0.0.1, ::1) are accepted.
  Error message updated to match ("must target bigquery.googleapis.com").
- connectors/bigquery/src/connector.rs updates the `base_url_policy`
  rustdoc to match the new policy, removes the
  `base_url_policy_accepts_other_googleapis` test (which asserted the old,
  too-permissive behavior), and fixes the
  `base_url_policy_rejects_unknown_host` assertion to check for the
  tightened error text.
- connectors/bigquery/manifest.toml removes `*.googleapis.com` from the
  per-operation `host_allow` blocks so host enforcement at the manifest
  layer matches the code policy. Also declares the new `bigquery.*.*`
  optional capabilities so granular tokens (datasets.read, jobs.read,
  jobs.write, tables.read) can be issued independently from the coarse
  `bigquery.*` capability.

No functional regression for real callers: every observed operation in
crates/fcp-e2e/tests/bigquery_compliance_e2e.rs and the local test
fixtures goes through `bigquery.googleapis.com` or `localhost`.

files.patch:
@@ -43,7 +43,12 @@ required = [
     "network.tls.sni",
     "storage.state",
 ]
-optional = []
+optional = [
+    "bigquery.datasets.read",
+    "bigquery.jobs.read",
+    "bigquery.jobs.write",
+    "bigquery.tables.read",
+]
 forbidden = [
     "system.exec",
     "network.listen",
@@ -76,7 +81,6 @@ type = "array"
 [provides.operations."bigquery.datasets.list".network_constraints]
 host_allow = [
     "bigquery.googleapis.com",
-    "*.googleapis.com",
 ]
 port_allow = [443]
 ip_allow = []
@@ -125,7 +129,6 @@ type = "array"
 [provides.operations."bigquery.jobs.list".network_constraints]
 host_allow = [
     "bigquery.googleapis.com",
-    "*.googleapis.com",
 ]
 port_allow = [443]
 ip_allow = []
@@ -183,7 +186,6 @@ type = "array"
 [provides.operations."bigquery.jobs.query".network_constraints]
 host_allow = [
     "bigquery.googleapis.com",
-    "*.googleapis.com",
 ]
 port_allow = [443]
 ip_allow = []
@@ -237,7 +239,6 @@ type = "array"
 [provides.operations."bigquery.tables.list".network_constraints]
 host_allow = [
     "bigquery.googleapis.com",
-    "*.googleapis.com",
 ]
 port_allow = [443]
 ip_allow = []
